title: Fix role registration flow and role-based routing (frontend-only)

requirements.summary: Ensure traveller/driver registration succeeds without frontend crashes, displays backend failure messages, and navigates to the correct dashboard after success.
acceptanceCriteria: When signed in with Internet Identity, clicking “Register as Traveller” results in a backend RegistrationStatus success and the app navigates to the Traveller dashboard.
When signed in with Internet Identity, registering as Driver with a valid bus number results in RegistrationStatus success and the app navigates to the Driver dashboard.
If registration fails, the UI shows the exact failure message returned by the backend (RegistrationStatus.failure) and does not navigate away.
No registration attempt results in an unhandled exception or blank screen in the frontend.

file_operations.description: Add a small helper to normalize/unwrap backend call errors (including trapped/rejected calls) into a user-facing English message so registration can always surface a readable error instead of crashing. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Harden registration-related React Query mutations and the getCallerUserProfile query against thrown errors: (1) ensure mutations consistently return a RegistrationStatus-shaped result or throw a normalized Error using the shared backend error helper, (2) ensure registration success invalidates/refetches currentUserProfile, and (3) prevent query errors from causing blank-screen behavior by handling expected authorization/registration-not-found failures gracefully. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Update admin/driver/traveller registration handlers to: (1) rely on RegistrationStatus for success/failure messaging (show exact failure string), (2) only navigate after confirming the refreshed caller profile indicates the expected role (driver via non-empty busNumber; traveller otherwise; admin via role), and (3) use the shared backend error helper for non-RegistrationStatus exceptions to avoid unhandled errors/blank screens. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Adjust post-auth routing so it waits for a stable profile fetch after registration invalidation and routes deterministically based on the refreshed profile, without relying on brittle immediate navigation. Also ensure any errors from profile fetching do not crash rendering. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Make frontend role interpretation and routing compatible with the candid-generated UserProfile.role type and reliable across admin/driver/traveller.
acceptanceCriteria: After successful Traveller registration, getCallerUserProfile returns a non-null profile that the frontend interprets as a traveller and routes to the Traveller dashboard.
After successful Driver registration, getCallerUserProfile returns a non-null profile with a non-null busNumber and the frontend routes to the Driver dashboard.
After successful Admin registration (when permitted), getCallerUserProfile indicates an admin role and the frontend routes to the Admin dashboard.
App.tsx does not compare role values in a way that can never be true for the actual candid-generated role type.

file_operations.description: Update role checks to use the generated UserRole enum (e.g., UserRole.admin) instead of raw string literals, and centralize the 'determine dashboard' logic (admin vs driver via busNumber vs traveller) to match the generated types. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Align header role display and dashboard navigation logic with the same generated UserRole enum and shared role-to-dashboard rules used by App.tsx, so UI labels and navigation match the actual candid-generated role values. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

After successful registration, use the refreshed getCallerUserProfile result to decide navigation (admin via role enum; driver via non-empty busNumber; traveller otherwise) to avoid mismatches when backend role encodings change. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
